headline: Functionland Hits 900 Nodes as DePIN Enters Revenue Era
subtitle: With a growing plugin ecosystem and a redesigned consumer app, Functionland is building the infrastructure habits the broader DePIN sector now demands.
briefing:
Functionland's Fula network enters May 2026 with 900 active nodes, a refreshed FxFiles app, and its first live third-party revenue plugin — a posture that aligns well with where the DePIN industry is heading. <cite index="21-7">In January 2026, decentralized physical infrastructure networks crossed $150 million in monthly on-chain revenue from customers paying for actual services — compute cycles, storage deals, and bandwidth — because it was cheaper and faster than the alternative.</cite> That milestone has reset analyst expectations: <cite index="22-16,22-19">winning networks are now judged by revenue per active node and utilization rates, as the market has transitioned from rewarding novel ideas to demanding concrete metrics.</cite>

Functionland's recent product moves reflect exactly that discipline. The Olostep plugin — a web-data API for AI teams — gives FxBlox node operators a direct path to external revenue rather than relying solely on token emissions. Meanwhile, the relaunched FxFiles app introduces one-click NFT and website generation with no Web3 knowledge required, directly addressing the consumer-adoption barrier that most DePIN projects have yet to solve. <cite index="11-13">Fula storage is already priced at up to 2x cheaper than centralized providers and up to 10x cheaper than decentralized alternatives</cite> — a cost advantage that is increasingly meaningful as <cite index="21-11">major US technology companies are expected to pour roughly $650 billion into AI infrastructure in 2026, a 36 percent increase over 2025.</cite>

<cite index="12-1">In Q2 2026, Functionland's roadmap targets migrating larger companies and organizations' data from traditional cloud to the Fula ecosystem</cite> — a move that, if executed, would mark the project's clearest step yet from consumer DePIN toward enterprise infrastructure. Watch whether that enterprise migration effort produces verifiable paid-usage metrics over the coming quarter.
